customTitle: Forked: Проблема вставки в удаленном доступе

message.text: Вопрос по поводу вставки в буфер текста после распознавания. Ошибка следующая. Когда я работаю в Microsoft Today, я захожу на Windows машину и пытаюсь делать распознавание, то в конце распознавания в интерфейс в удаленном доступе вставляется просто буква "V", латинская маленькая, то есть скорее всего связано с Ctrl+V и мне приходится возвращаться в экран макбука из удаленного рабочего стола Windows, на который я захожу из макбука, то есть я возвращаюсь в экран макбука, выбираю блокнот, делаю опять "Command+V", то есть вставку вставляется в макбуке распознанный текст, делаю Ctrl+C копирование и тогда это уже успешно вставляется, возвращаясь в удаленный доступ и успешно там вставляюсь. Это дело починить, чтобы в экран удаленного доступа тоже вставлялась содержимое буфера.

response.value:
The user is describing a paste issue when working in Microsoft Remote Desktop (accessing a Windows machine from macOS). When transcription completes, instead of pasting the text, only the letter "v" gets inserted into the remote desktop window. This is because the paste mechanism uses `Cmd+V` (or `Ctrl+V`) keystroke simulation, but when the remote desktop has focus, the keystrokes get sent to the remote machine differently.

Let me look at the current paste/insertion logic in init.lua.
response.generatedTitle: Analyzed paste issue in Microsoft Remote Desktop

response.value:
The issue is clear: when paste mode is used, `hs.eventtap.keyStroke({"cmd"}, 9)` sends Cmd+V. But when a remote desktop window (like Microsoft Remote Desktop) has focus, the Cmd key modifier may not be captured properly, and only "v" gets sent.

The problem: `hs.eventtap.keyStroke` sends keyboard events at the macOS level. When Microsoft Remote Desktop is the focused app, it intercepts keystrokes and forwards them to the Windows machine. But `cmd+v` might be forwarded as just `v` to the Windows machine, because the remote desktop app might not translate Cmd+V to Ctrl+V.
So the fix is to detect when Microsoft Remote Desktop is the active application and send Ctrl+V instead of Cmd+V, since that's what Windows expects for paste operations.
response.generatedTitle: Analyzed paste mode issue with Microsoft Remote Desktop

response.value: Проблема ясна: при вставке используется `Cmd+V`, но Microsoft Remote Desktop перехватывает клавиши и пересылает их в Windows — Cmd не транслируется в Ctrl, поэтому Windows получает просто `v`. Нужно определять, когда активно приложение удалённого рабочего стола, и использовать `Ctrl+V` вместо `Cmd+V`.

edits.text:
Bundle IDs of remote desktop / VM apps that require Ctrl+V instead of Cmd+V
local REMOTE_DESKTOP_BUNDLE_IDS = {
    ["com.microsoft.rdc.macos"]   = true,  -- Microsoft Remote Desktop
    ["com.microsoft.rdc.mac"]     = true,  -- older Microsoft Remote Desktop
    ["com.parallels.desktop"]     = true,  -- Parallels Desktop
    ["org.virtualbox.app.VirtualBox"] = true,  -- VirtualBox
    ["com.vmware.fusion"]         = true,  -- VMware Fusion
}

local function frontAppUsesCtrlV()
    local app = hs.application.frontmostApplication()
    return app and REMOTE_DESKTOP_BUNDLE_IDS[app:bundleID()] or false
end

-- Low-level text insertion at cursor
local function insertTextAtCursor(text, mode)
    if mode == "paste" then
        -- Note: we intentionally don't save/restore clipboard — getContents() can block
        -- for 60+ seconds if another app holds a large object on the clipboard.
        hs.pasteboard.setContents(text)
        if frontAppUsesCtrlV() then
            -- Remote desktop apps forward Ctrl+V to the guest OS (Windows paste shortcut).
            -- Cmd+V would arrive as bare "v" on the remote side.
            hs.eventtap.keyStroke({"ctrl"}, "v")
        else
            hs.eventtap.keyStroke({"cmd"}, 9)  -- keycode 9 = V (ANSI), works regardless of keyboard layout
        end

response.value:
The issue is that `hs.eventtap.keyStroke({"ctrl"}, "v")` is also being intercepted by the remote desktop app and forwarded as `Ctrl+V` to Windows, but maybe it's sending it wrong. 

Actually, let me think differently. The real issue might be that `hs.pasteboard.setContents(text)` sets the macOS clipboard, but the remote desktop app has its own clipboard sync mechanism. When Ctrl+V is pressed inside the remote desktop, it uses the Windows clipboard, not the macOS clipboard.

So the fundamental problem is: **clipboard is not being shared between macOS and Windows Remote Desktop in real-time**. 

The macOS clipboard gets updated, but by the time the keystroke happens, the Windows side hasn't synced yet. Instead of relying on the clipboard, I could use character-by-character typing to send the text directly through keystrokes, which the remote desktop will forward as typing input. I should detect when a remote desktop app is active and automatically switch to this typing mode rather than pasting.

response.value:
The user wants to:
1. Remove the remote desktop detection logic entirely
2. Understand why clipboard doesn't sync to Windows Remote Desktop

The user says clipboard sharing between macOS and Windows RDP works normally (they can copy-paste manually), but when Hammerspoon sets the clipboard via `hs.pasteboard.setContents()`, Windows doesn't get it.

The likely reason: `hs.pasteboard.setContents()` sets the clipboard, then immediately fires `Cmd+V` (or the next line). The RDP clipboard sync is event-driven and needs a moment to propagate. The `Cmd+V` fires before Windows has synced the new clipboard content.
I'll add a delay between setting the clipboard and triggering the paste keystroke to give Windows time to sync, then strip out the remote desktop detection code.

response.value:
The user is saying that when Hammerspoon uses `hs.pasteboard.setContents()`, the content ends up in the macOS clipboard but doesn't propagate to the Windows RDP clipboard at all - even after waiting. Only when the user manually types Ctrl+C on the Mac keyboard does it go to the Windows clipboard.

This is interesting. Let me think about this...

In macOS, there are multiple pasteboards:
- `NSGeneralPasteboard` - the general pasteboard (Cmd+C/Cmd+V)
- `NSFindPasteboard` - find pasteboard
- etc.

But more importantly, `hs.pasteboard.setContents()` uses `NSPasteboard` which is the general pasteboard. 

RDP clipboard sync likely monitors the pasteboard change count to detect when content changes, but maybe Microsoft Remote Desktop only recognizes clipboard updates triggered by actual user keyboard shortcuts rather than programmatic changes. The pasteboard owner concept might be key here - when an app sets clipboard content directly, it becomes the owner, but RDP might only sync when it detects a traditional copy operation.

response.value:
Interesting! I found the relevant GitHub issue: "hs.eventtap.keyStrokes only send aaaa with RDP" - that explains the 'aaa...' problem the user experienced.

Now about the clipboard not syncing: the key insight from the Apple docs is that `NSGeneralPasteboard` "automatically participates with the Universal Clipboard feature." But that's for Handoff between Apple devices, not Windows RDP.

For Microsoft Remote Desktop clipboard sync, I need to understand how it works. RDP clipboard sync typically works by monitoring clipboard changes on the host (macOS). The Microsoft Remote Desktop app monitors the macOS clipboard change count and when it changes, syncs to Windows.
The problem with `hs.pasteboard.setContents()` is that it might not trigger the clipboard change notification that RDP's monitor is listening for, or Hammerspoon's process doesn't properly signal ownership of the clipboard in a way that RDP recognizes. The fact that the user said it doesn't sync even after 10 seconds rules out a simple timing issue—this points to `hs.pasteboard.setContents()` not firing the clipboard change event that RDP depends on. The real issue might be that RDP only syncs the clipboard when the user interacts with the RDP window, not continuously in the background. When a user manually copies on Mac and then pastes in RDP, the sync happens at that moment of user activity. But when Hammerspoon programmatically updates the clipboard, RDP never gets triggered to sync because there's no user action inside the RDP window to prompt it.

I'm also wondering if Hammerspoon's pasteboard API isn't properly declaring itself as the clipboard owner in a way that RDP recognizes as a legitimate clipboard change. # Architecture: Hammerspoon-only (no Karabiner, no bash scripts at runtime)
set -euo pipefail

# ─── Colors ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
───
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
--
HAMMERSPOON_DIR="$HOME/.hammerspoon"

# ─── Preflight ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
───
echo ""
echo -e "${BOLD}local-whisper installer${NC}"
echo -e "Hold a key → speak → release → text at cursor"
--
if brew list --cask hammerspoon &>/dev/null; then
    ok "hammerspoon already installed"
else
    info "Installing hammerspoon..."
    brew install --cask hammerspoon
    ok "hammerspoon installed"
fi

# ─── Step 2: Build whisper.cpp ────────────────────────────────────────────
───
echo ""
info "Step 2/6: Building whisper.cpp..."
--
# ─── Step 4: Install Hammerspoon config ───────────────────────────────────
──
echo ""
info "Step 4/6: Setting up Hammerspoon..."

mkdir -p "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR"

# Create config directory for user settings
CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.local-whisper"
mkdir -p "$CONFIG_DIR"
ok "Config directory: $CONFIG_DIR"
--
if [[ -f "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/init.lua" ]]; then
    if grep -q "local-whisper" "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/init.lua"; then
        # Existing local-whisper config — update it but preserve user settin
gs
        # (user settings live in ~/.local-whisper/, not in init.lua)
        cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/hammerspoon/init.lua" "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/init.lua"
        ok "Hammerspoon config updated"
    else
        warn "Existing init.lua found — backing up to init.lua.backup"
        cp "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/init.lua" "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/init.lua.backup"
        cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/hammerspoon/init.lua" "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/init.lua"
        ok "Hammerspoon config installed (backup saved)"
    fi
else
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/hammerspoon/init.lua" "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/init.lua"
    ok "Hammerspoon config installed"
fi

# Install example voice commands if user doesn't have a config yet
if [[ ! -f "$HAMMERSPOON_DIR/local_whisper_actions.lua" ]]; then

MERSPOON_DIR/local_whisper_actions.lua"

er_actions.lua to customize)"
